mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO is rising, or Spearman correlation between INDPRO change and 20-day return is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that contracts equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls signal robust consumer demand and income growth that jointly drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative when both RSAFS and PAYEMS rise month-over-month in more than 45% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental R-squared in a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during late-cycle expansions signals deteriorating household income and slowing consumption, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered late-cycle instances, or the signal fails to outperform a naive short strategy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2.5% signals aggregate demand outperformance that supports corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the mean triggered return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling housing starts signal a credit-sensitive demand contraction that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases over prior 3 months and HOUST decreases over prior 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises and HOUST falls concurrently, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI acceleration above 0.4% signals intensifying inflation pressure that erodes real earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the negative mean return in triggered periods is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in housing starts exceeding 100 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 100 thousand units over 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered pre-recession instances, or the signal fails to generate negative average returns over a 5-year rolling window.
